purpose: Define the permanent macOS SVGA Workbench information architecture without starting Phase 2 or later product capabilities.
productPrinciple: Auto-SVGA is a local SVGA acceptance and refinement workbench, not a mini After Effects, not a Figma SVGA Editor clone, and not a full animation creation MVP.

regions.label: Source / Document
regions.currentScope: Local file and mode context only.

regions.label: Preview Stage
regions.currentScope: Local SVGA playback, A/B preview, export-review reference preview.

regions.label: Inspector
regions.currentScope: Readable overview, current file facts, and report-backed diagnostics.

regions.label: Resources
regions.currentScope: Designer-readable resource rows and sequence grouping surface; no editing or optimization actions.

regions.label: Action / Workflow
regions.currentScope: Preview, inspector, activity, theme, and settings controls.

regions.label: Activity / History
regions.currentScope: User-readable local activity and bounded diagnostic details.

deferredFeaturePolicy.phase2: Reserve diagnostic capacity only; do not expose inactive optimization controls.
deferredFeaturePolicy.phase3: Reserve replaceable-element editing capacity only; do not expose inactive imageKey or text editing controls.
deferredFeaturePolicy.phase4: Reserve sequence-review capacity only; do not expose anti-flicker or scheduling controls.
deferredFeaturePolicy.midTerm: Adapters may attach to the same regions later, but no multi-format parser or converter starts in P6-R1.
deferredFeaturePolicy.longTerm: Agent, Motion Plan, ComfyUI, and generation modules remain isolated future work requiring separate approval.

ownerVisibleRules: Preview Stage remains the visual anchor.
Resources must be designer-readable and ready for grouping.
Diagnostics are progressive disclosure, not the default user face.
Activity is user-readable and may reveal details only on demand.
Settings defaults to local preview; export/latest-artifact/debug options stay advanced.
